cards.back: A software testing method where the tester has no knowledge of the internal workings of the system, and tests it based on the inputs and expected outputs.
A software testing method where the tester has access to the internal workings of the system, and tests it based on the code and expected outputs.
A software development approach that emphasizes flexibility, collaboration, and rapid delivery, with iterative and incremental releases.
A software testing method where individual units of code are tested in isolation to ensure they function as expected, and are integrated into the larger system.

A software development practice that involves integrating code changes into a central repository frequently, and automatically deploying them to production, to ensure rapid delivery and minimize downtime.
